question.text: Under the presumptive taxation scheme (Section 44AD), what is the profit rate declared for exchange-traded derivatives income received through banking channels (digital/online modes)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The text states: "Under the presumptive scheme, the investor can choose to declare the profits at the rate of 6% of turnover as the payment is always received through banking channels." It also notes: "he can declare the profit at the rate of 6% of such turnover in case of receipts in cheque or any digital modes or 8% of turnover in case of cash receipts." **The Trap:** 8% applies to cash receipts. Since exchange-traded derivatives involve only banking/digital channels, the applicable rate is 6%, not 8%. **Cross-Exam Context:** Section 44AD presumptive scheme: turnover limit Rs. 2 crores (basic); increased to Rs. 3 crores if 95% of receipts are through online modes (Union Budget FY 2024-25 amendment). Under this scheme, no books of accounts required, and 100% advance tax can be paid by March 15. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** 6% for digital/banking (exchange-traded derivatives always qualify); 8% for cash. ETCD = always 6% since settlement is only through banking channels.
